_meta.description: Cortex Lite anchor settings dataset. 10 games x 3 (goal, gpu_tier) combinations = 30 records. Each record is calibration ground truth for the HeuristicRecommender, not a universal lookup table. gpu_tier values follow the project's absolute-threshold tiers (low < 8000 G3D Mark, mid 8000-13999, high 14000-21999). steam_app_id is null for games not distributed on Steam -- these must be added to a user's library as manual entries (source='manual'), not via Steam sync.

presets.notes: Source: Digital Foundry PC settings breakdown (via TheGamer, PC Gamer). DF found SSR and ambient occlusion give minimal visual return for their cost and recommends leaving both low even on high-end rigs, so that guidance carries down into the low tier too.

Source: Digital Foundry PC settings breakdown; PC Gamer 2.0/Phantom Liberty settings guide. Shadow and volumetric settings pushed higher than perf tier since DF found shadow-family settings scale well with little frame cost; SSR/AO held low per the same DF finding used at every tier.

Source: PC Gamer 'best settings for Cyberpunk 2077 2.0 and Phantom Liberty'. DLSS recommended over FSR/XeSS as the sharpest, most stable upscaler on Nvidia hardware. RT reserved for high tier since PC Gamer frames full path tracing as too costly for anything below top-end hardware.

Source: Switchblade Gaming CS2 low-end PC guide. Global Shadow Quality is called out as the single largest FPS lever in Source 2 (20-35 FPS swing on GTX 1060-class hardware); 4:3 stretched is the long-standing competitive convention for wider player models. Shader Detail is kept high regardless of tier since it only costs ~3 FPS but avoids on-the-fly shader compilation stutter.

Source: recharge.com 'Best CS2 Settings 2026' competitive guide. Boost Player Contrast and Global Shadow Quality at Medium+ are called out as visibility-relevant, not just cosmetic, so they're kept on even in a performance-leaning config.

Source: recharge.com competitive settings guide; Eloking CS2 low/mid/high breakdown. Ambient Occlusion stays disabled even at the 'quality' anchor -- multiple sources agree it can obscure enemies in smoke-heavy fights, so it's one of the few settings this dataset deliberately does not raise with GPU tier.

Source: Digital Trends Elden Ring PC performance guide. Elden Ring has no native DLSS/FSR/XeSS; Digital Trends' own optimized settings (31% gain over Max preset) leave shadow and SSAO as the highest-value cuts, matching this record.

Source: PCGamesN 'best Elden Ring settings for max fps'; Switchblade Gaming Elden Ring 2026 guide. PCGamesN recommends the High preset over Max for most players since Max's gains are marginal; texture quality is matched to VRAM tier rather than GPU tier since it's VRAM-bound, not FPS-bound, per Switchblade.

Source: Switchblade Gaming Elden Ring 2026 guide. Ray tracing costs ~15-25% FPS on RTX 4070-class hardware, framed as worthwhile only if the 60fps cap still holds -- included here since 'high' tier is exactly the hardware class where that tradeoff clears.

Source: Switchblade Gaming Valorant low-end PC guide. Not a Steam title (Riot launcher only) -- games.metadata_status for this record must come from a manual library entry, not Steam sync. Material Quality Low recovers ~80-110 FPS on GTX 1660-class hardware per Switchblade's PC settings guide.

Source: PCGamesN 'best Valorant settings for optimized PC performance'. Material Quality stays Low even at mid/high tiers -- PCGamesN and Switchblade both frame it as a rendering-cost cut with no gameplay-relevant visual loss, not a quality tradeoff, so raising it with tier isn't justified.

Source: Switchblade Gaming 'Best Valorant PC settings 2026'. Even at the high-end anchor, competitive-shooter convention keeps Material Quality low; PCGamesN's own RTX 4070 test rig ran these settings at 305fps at 4K, underscoring how little headroom this game actually needs.

Source: pcoptimizedsettings.com BG3 optimization guide; PCGamesN BG3 settings guide. Ambient Occlusion, Subsurface Scattering, and Dynamic Crowds are each called out as small FPS wins for real visual cost, and BG3 is noted as CPU-bottlenecked even at low settings, so Dynamic Crowds (NPC AI load) is disabled first.

Source: PCGamesN BG3 settings guide; ROG Ally BG3 performance guide. Dynamic Crowds is specifically flagged as necessary to disable in Act 3 (Baldur's Gate city) regardless of tier, since NPC density there is what causes the CPU bottleneck, not the GPU-side settings.

Source: Digital Trends BG3 performance guide. Digital Trends measured Ultra vs Low at only a 10-12% FPS difference at 4K due to the CPU bottleneck -- called out explicitly since it means high-tier GPUs get comparatively little payoff from maxing every slider here versus other anchor games.

Source: Switchblade Gaming Fortnite low-end PC guide. Not a Steam title (Epic Games Store only) -- requires a manual library entry, same caveat as Valorant. Performance Mode swaps in pre-built low-poly assets and is described as roughly doubling FPS on GTX 1650-class hardware.

Source: Switchblade Gaming Fortnite PC settings 2026 guide. View Distance is kept at Epic even in a performance-leaning config since visibility of distant players/builds is treated as a competitive necessity distinct from other quality settings, which stay low.

Source: Digital Trends Fortnite PC performance guide. Digital Trends explicitly recommends leaving ray tracing, reflections, and Nanite geometry off even on high-end hardware for competitive play, since they don't improve target-spotting and cost meaningful FPS -- so this anchor keeps RT off even at the quality tier, unlike the other anchor games.

Source: Switchblade Gaming Minecraft Java FPS guide. Not distributed on Steam (Java Edition is sold via minecraft.net / Microsoft Store only) -- requires a manual library entry. Switchblade calls render distance the single biggest FPS lever, and separately notes Java is CPU-bound: an i5-13600K with a GTX 1060 outperforms an i3-12100 with an RTX 3070 in practice, so this game's tiering leans more on CPU tier than GPU tier despite gpu_tier being the anchor key.

Source: Apex Hosting simulation distance guide; Switchblade Gaming Minecraft Java FPS guide. Simulation distance is kept lower than render distance deliberately -- Apex Hosting frames this as the standard balance since it controls background tick load (mob AI, farms) separately from what's simply visible.

Source: Switchblade Gaming Minecraft Java FPS guide. Shaders are flagged optional rather than default even at the quality anchor, since Java's single-thread-bound render pipeline means a high GPU tier doesn't guarantee shader headroom -- CPU tier matters more, echoing the performance-tier note above.

Source: AVG GTA V performance guide; Switchblade Gaming GTA V Enhanced 2026 guide. Reflects GTA V Enhanced (the March 2025 rebuild that replaced the original PC release on Steam/Epic) rather than the discontinued Legacy version. Grass Quality and MSAA are called out as the two highest-value FPS cuts.

Source: Steam Community 'GTA V Enhanced: Ultimate Graphics & Optimization Guide'. Texture Quality is treated as effectively free at this tier (set as high as VRAM allows) since it's VRAM-bound rather than FPS-bound, consistent with the pattern seen in RDR2 and Elden Ring.

settings.upscaling: DLSS Quality mode (DLSS 4 Frame Gen on RTX 40/50)

presets.notes: Source: Switchblade Gaming 'Best GTA V PC Settings 2026'. Upscaling is described as the single highest-impact setting in GTA V Enhanced even at the quality tier -- DLSS Quality mode is framed as visually indistinguishable from native at normal viewing distances while recovering GPU headroom for ray tracing.

Source: Steam Community 'RDR2 Optimized Settings' (Xbox One X-equivalent breakdown, cross-checked against Digital Foundry's settings analysis). Reflection Quality dropping to Low nets an 8-9% FPS gain per pcoptimizedsettings.com's benchmarks, for minimal visible loss -- one of the clearest cost/benefit cuts in the whole dataset.

Source: pcoptimizedsettings.com 'RDR2 Optimization: Best RDR2 Settings'. Reflection Quality is kept at Low even at the mid/balanced anchor, deliberately breaking the usual 'raise everything with tier' pattern, because pcoptimizedsettings.com found the visual cost of higher reflection settings isn't justified at any but the highest tier.

Source: pcoptimizedsettings.com RDR2 optimization guide; NeoGAF thread compiling Digital Foundry's full RDR2 settings breakdown. Reflection Quality is raised only to Medium even here, not Ultra/ ultra -- consistent with DF's finding that it's among the most expensive settings in the game for the least visual return.

Source: pcoptimizedsettings.com Helldivers 2 optimized settings; Hone.gg Helldivers 2 FPS guide. Helldivers 2 shipped without DLSS/FSR (a basic spatial upscaler is used instead), and is described as fundamentally CPU-bound -- Async Compute is disabled since multiple sources found it costs up to 20% FPS with no visual benefit, on any tier.

Source: Steam Community 'Helldivers 2 Optimized Graphics Presets'; pcoptimizedsettings.com. Render Scale 'Ultra Quality' (not Native) is the specific recommendation multiple sources converge on for mid-tier hardware -- described as a ~40% FPS gain for minimal visible loss, which is why it's used here instead of Native.

Source: pcoptimizedsettings.com Helldivers 2 optimized settings. Reflection Quality is kept at Low even at the quality anchor -- pcoptimizedsettings.com's own benchmarks show High reflections cost ~9% FPS versus Lowest for a visual difference the guide itself calls minimal, so this is one of the few settings this dataset does not raise at the top tier.
